subject: BIOL
course_description: This course is designed for seniors who are currently conducting laboratory research and will apply for graduate school in STEM. We begin with writing a fellowship application and conclude with oral and visual communication, including giving engaging slide-based talks.
course_notes: (This course is also taught as IBS 510.) This course is designed for undergraduate seniors who are currently conducting laboratory research and are likely applying for graduate school in STEM in the next year of two. We will begin with written science communication, crafting a fellowship application for the National Science Foundation. Students do not have to be eligible to submit the fellowship to enroll in the course. We will conclude with oral and visual communication, including giving engaging slide-based talks, compelling data presentation, accessibility considerations, and communication to a non-scientific audience. The course will involve multiple rounds of feedback and iteration on deliverables (e.g., fellowship, presentation), and may involve a considerable amount of time outside of class, especially during the first half of the semester. BIOL 310W satisfies both a Biology elective and a GER continued writing requirement. We also welcome enrollment by first- and second-year PhD students, who should enroll in the graduate version of the course, IBS 510. Undergraduates may also enroll in IBS 510 to satisfy their graduate-level Honors requirement. Enrollment is by instructor permission only. Contact Dr. Leila Rieder at leila.rieder@emory.edu for permission.
requisites: (BIOL 142 and 142L) or BIOL_OX 142 or equivalent transfer credit as prerequisites.
